rulings.Case Title: JULIE ANN NEYMAN VS. DE JAVU-SAN FRANCISCO, LLC. CA. DBA DEJA VU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Against Bsc Management Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 10, 2010, line 2. DEFENDANT DEJA VU SAN FRANCISCO, LLC DBA DEJA VU'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Against B S C Management Inc. Granted. The entry of the default against B S C was a mistake of the clerk of the court. Default was entered while the case was stayed. B S C prevailed at arbitration. The judgment shall be amended.

rulings.Case Title: VIOLET FONG HOM VS. CYNTHIA JONE FONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 10, 2010, line 3. DEFENDANT CYNTHIA FONG, GENEVIEVE NG'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint. Denied. Insufficient showing of diligence, evidence was in cross-complainants possession since 2007. Prejudice demonstrated by cross-defendant in that motion for summary judgment has been filed to be heard in January, and upcoming trial date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MILLER et al VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 10, 2010, line 12. Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Denied without prejudice. November 29, 2010 ex parte order instructed K2 Environmental to file a motion for determination of good faith settlement and to provide a courtesy copy of that filing. K2 Environmental never filed that motion and the Court has never been provided any evidence that would support a finding of good faith per Tech-Bilt. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ARASH GHANADAN VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, Officer Larry Bertrand And Chief Of Police George Gascon'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 10, 2010, line 11. Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, Officer Larry Bertrand And Chief Of Police George Gascon's Motion For Summary Judgment. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied. Defendant shifts burden, plaintiff raises triable issues of material fact: (1) when plaintiff was arrested; (2) whether probable cause existed for the arrest; and (3) whether Officer Bertrand may have known that plaintiff was (or was not) in charge of the party. (Bertrand deposition, Evans declaration, Ghezlaoui declaration). Triable issue of fact raised whether plaintiff's legal right to file a complaint with the O C C was interfered with by the "Now yoll you'll see what happens..." comment. (declaration of Homidi). Plaintiff may plead intentional torts and negligence in the alternative. Triable issue of fact raised whether the C C S F and Police Chief Gascon retained Officer Bertrand knowing that he posed a danger. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EDITH MAZZAFERRI VS. RONALD MAZZAFERRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Strike Pltf'S Complaint Under The Anti - Slapp Statutes
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 10, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT DANIELLE DUPERRET'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Complaint Under The Anti - Slapp Statutes. Denied. The gravamen of this action concerns recovering trust assets and quieting title and does not arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. Plaintiff demonstrates a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. The court finds the special motion to strike is frivolous and is solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. Defendants, and each of them are jointly and severally ordered to play plaintiff reasonable attorney fees and costs in the amount of $11,150. C.C.P. section 425.16(c). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 10, 2010, line 7. Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Off calendar. Notice of removal filed on November 30, 2010. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY B. EPSTEIN VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER, GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Plaintiff'S/Petitioners Mtn For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 10, 2010, line 8. INTERVENOR DONALD CASPER'S Joinder In Plaintiff'S/Petitioners Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Denied. Plaintiff is unlikely to prevail on the merits for the reasons set forth in the above tentative ruling. Additionally, there has been no illegal delegation of authority. The legislature was specific in its delegation, identified specific buildings, and stated the price of fair market value or greater. The legislature required defendant to report back before the deal was consummated. See Salmon Trollers Marketing Association, Inc. v. Fullerton (1981) 124 Cal App 3d 291. The "Best interests of the state" direction is permissible. Insufficient showing that the balancing of hardships favors plaintiffs. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY B. EPSTEIN VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER, GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Application Of Plaintiffs Jerry Bv. Epstein And A. Redmond Doms.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 10, 2010, line 8. Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Application Of Plaintiffs Jerry B. Epstein And A. Redmond Doms. Denied. Plaintiffs are unlikely to prevail on the merits. As taxpayers, plaintiffs lack sufficient standing to obtain a preliminary injunction. Cohen v. Bd. of Supervisors (1986) 178 Cal. App. 3d 447. White v. Davis (2003) 30 Cal 4th 528. Plaintiffs do not demonstrate waste under Sundance v. Municipal Court (1986) 42 Cal. 3d 1101 or an illegal gift under Community Memorial Hospital v. County of Ventura (1996) 50 Cal. App. 4th 199. Selling the buildings does not violate G.C. section 69204 (Judicial Council exercises authority). G.C. section 14670.13 states that "notwithstanding any other law", the sale can go forward. G.C. section 69204 does not designate the Judicial Council as the owners of the buildings, and its authorization is not required. Selling the buildings does not implicate the separation of powers doctrine or affect a core function of the judicial branch. See Superior Court of Mendocino v. County of Mendocino (1996) 13 Cal 4th 45. The court will not interfere with the discretion of the judicial and legislative branches. Plaintiff's request to present oral testimony is denied. =(302/CWW)
